title: Sign manifest with Ed25519 key (generated on first run)
completion_summary: src/apprentice_trainer/manifest_signer.py + apprentice-sign console script. ensure_keypair() generates a 32+32-byte Ed25519 keypair at ~/.apprentice/keys/{ed25519.priv,ed25519.pub} on first run; private key written via os.open(..., 0o600) BEFORE rename so it's never world-readable even for an instant; idempotent on second call. sign_manifest() reads the manifest's raw bytes (deterministic byte stream guaranteed by manifest_writer's sort_keys=True), signs with the private key, writes <manifest>.sig as raw 64 bytes. verify_manifest() returns True/False (also False with helpful logs if .sig or pubkey is missing). CLI: `apprentice-sign sign <m>` / `apprentice-sign verify <m>` (exit 0/1) / `apprentice-sign keygen` / `apprentice-sign show-pubkey` (base64). 10 new pytest cases (38 total now, all pass on CPU): keypair generated, private key 0600, keygen idempotent, sign-verify roundtrip, verify rejects tampering, signatures byte-identical for same input (the acceptance bullet -- falls out of Ed25519's deterministic spec + manifest_writer's deterministic JSON), verify returns False on missing sig / missing pubkey, CLI sign+verify integration, CLI verify exits 1 on tampering. End-to-end smoke run: sign -> verify (rc=0) -> append a byte -> verify (rc=1). cryptography>=42 added to pyproject.toml. Deliverables: trainer/src/apprentice_trainer/manifest_signer.py + tests/test_manifest_signer.py + apprentice-sign script + the runtime artifacts (keys/ + manifest.sig).
acceptance_criteria: Ed25519 keypair generated on first run, stored at ~/.apprentice/keys/
Signature is deterministic for same input
